channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian government faces high costs for monitoring and supporting returning ISIS-affiliated women and children
item.description: Three Australian women linked to ISIS have recently returned from Syrian refugee camps to Sydney and Melbourne, sparking concerns over legal proceedings, security, and long-term rehabilitation. The wo...

item.title: Manitoba announces public health emergency due to increasing HIV cases
item.description: The Manitoba government has officially declared a public health emergency in response to a significant rise in HIV cases across the province. According to provincial health authorities, the number of ...

item.title: CAG Highlights Governance Challenges Hampering Urban Transport Efficiency
item.description: Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) Sanjay K Murthy has pointed out that governance, rather than infrastructure, is the main barrier to effective urban mobility in India. Speaking at a BRICS auditor...

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Operation Fails to Achieve Objectives
item.description: Donald Trump’s ‘Operation Project Freedom’, which was intended to guide stranded merchant vessels through the Strait of Hormuz and challenge Iranian control, has failed within a span of just 50 hours....

item.title: Magnitude-5.6 Earthquake Recorded in Davao Oriental, No Damage Expected
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — A magnitude-5.6 earthquake struck Davao Oriental on Wednesday night, according to the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS). The quake occurred at 11:32 p...

item.title: Mexico Records Significant Rise in Journalist Murders and Harassment in 2025
item.description: In 2025, Mexico experienced a sharp increase in violence against journalists, with eight reporters either murdered or disappeared, according to a report by the UK-based advocacy group Article 19. This...

item.title: Chinese Fugitive Arrested in Ogun for $245 Million Ponzi Scheme
item.description: Naija police don arrest one Chinese man wey dey run from law after he dey involve for big Ponzi scheme wey con people for over $245 million. The man, Xu Qing, wey dey wanted for China, don spend month...

item.title: 2026 Press Freedom Index Shows Global Decline in Media Independence
item.description: The 2026 World Press Freedom Index, compiled by Reporters Without Borders (RSF), reveals a troubling decline in global press freedom. For the first time, over half of the 180 countries surveyed fall i...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Highlights Low Public Risk
item.description: A rare hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship in the Atlantic has resulted in eight confirmed cases, including three deaths. The strain involved, the Andes virus, can spread between people,...

item.title: Strategies for Harnessing Africa’s Demographic Growth for Economic Development
item.description: Africa is experiencing an unprecedented demographic shift, with its population expected to reach 2.5 billion by 2050 and a median age of just 19, making it the youngest and most populous continent glo...

item.title: Strathmore Study Highlights Colorectal Cancer Challenges in Kenya
item.description: In Kenya, colorectal cancer (CRC) is becoming a serious health challenge, often detected too late and poorly understood. Strathmore University’s Centre for Research in Therapeutic Sciences (CREATES), ...

item.title: Authorities trace passengers after hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
item.description: Health authorities worldwide are urgently tracing passengers who disembarked from the MV Hondius cruise ship before a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed. At least 29 passengers from 12 countries left t...

item.title: NBI clarifies Mabanta arrested over extortion case, not politics
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has clarified that the arrest of political content creator Franco Mabanta and four co-respondents was based on strong evidence of extortion, not political mo...

item.title: TCS Nashik Sexual Harassment Case: Nida Khan Arrested by Police
item.description: Nida Khan, identified as a key accused in the sexual harassment and religious coercion case at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) BPO in Nashik, has been arrested by police from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar...

item.title: President Ruto Pushes for Full Digital Records to Fix Education System Irregularities
item.description: President William Ruto has called on the Ministry of Education to fully digitise all education records within the next two months. The move aims to boost accountability, transparency, and proper plann...

item.title: Fremantle Dockers come from behind to beat Hawthorn in thrilling AFL match
item.description: Fremantle Dockers have demonstrated remarkable resilience and skill by overcoming a 19-point deficit in the final quarter to defeat Hawthorn at Optus Stadium. Despite facing the challenge of three con...

item.title: Oshiomhole questions Akpabio's eligibility under revised Senate rules
item.description: Senator Adams Oshiomhole don talk say Senate President Godswill Akpabio no dey qualified to hold the office if we follow the new Senate leadership rules. According to Oshiomhole, the amended rules now...

item.title: Gen Z Canadians Travel to the U.S. Primarily Hindered by Cost and Lack of Paid Leave
item.description: A recent survey reveals that Gen Z Canadians (born 1997–2012) continue to show strong interest in travelling to the United States, with 45 per cent likely to visit next year. Among Canadians planning ...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship: What Australians Should Know
item.description: An outbreak of the Andes strain of hantavirus aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic has raised public concern, though health officials stress the risk to the general population remains low. The virus, ...

item.title: Haitian Immigrant Charged with Murder of Florida Store Clerk
item.description: Rolbert Joachin, a 40-year-old man who entered the United States illegally from Haiti, has been indicted on first-degree murder charges for the April 2 killing of Nilufa Easmın, a 51-year-old Banglade...
